behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels for attendee attribution
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires calling the real speech-to-text API with real audio to verify speaker diarization is actually returned and correctly extracted. Mocking the API would hide failures where the real API doesn't support speaker diarization in this mode, returns labels in an unexpected format, or the service fails to correctly parse and forward them. The real failure boundary is the external API: no mock can guarantee the API returns what we expect.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real speech-to-text API with multi-speaker test audio. Verify the transcript includes speaker identifiers (e.g., 'Speaker 1:', 'Speaker 2:') for each statement. Confirm the service preserves these labels when passing the transcript to the summarization step. Failure: if labels are missing, stripped, malformed, or not forwarded downstream.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires the real speech-to-text API because mocking would hide failures in real API format or behavior.

behaviors.description: Structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task
behaviors.justification: Whether the LLM correctly extracts all action items and attributes them to the right attendees depends on the real LLM's behavior, not mock data. Mocking the LLM would test only our JSON parsing logic on a fabricated response, not whether the real LLM actually identifies all action items from a transcript or handles edge cases like implicit assignments or multi-attendee tasks.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a real transcript with action items explicitly assigned to specific attendees to the real LLM with the summarization prompt. Verify the returned structured summary includes action items for every attendee who received an assignment. Failure: if the LLM misses action items, assigns them incorrectly, or fails to parse attendee names.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires the real LLM to verify it correctly extracts and attributes action items.

behaviors.description: Key decisions appear in the decisions section, not buried in narrative
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on the real LLM's ability to extract decisions into a dedicated section when given a prompt. Mocking the LLM would only test our JSON parsing on a synthetic response we invented. Real failure: the LLM ignores the prompt structure, buries decisions in narrative, or fails to identify decisions entirely. A mock cannot reveal prompt-compliance issues.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a transcript containing multiple clear decisions to the real LLM. Verify the 'decisions' field in the structured summary contains all key decisions, with none appearing only in the narrative field. Failure: if decisions are missing from the decisions field, buried in narrative, or the section is incomplete.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires the real LLM to verify it follows the prompt structure and correctly separates decisions from narrative.

behaviors.description: Audio longer than 60 minutes is segmented, processed sequentially, and results merged into coherent summary
behaviors.justification: This requires real audio >60 minutes, real speech-to-text API calls (which have real length limits), and real LLM calls to verify the merged summary is coherent. Mocking would hide failures in segmentation logic, API limit handling, segment ordering, or summary coherence. The primary failure is whether the merged output reads as a coherent whole—a property that only the real LLM can evaluate.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real audio >60 minutes (e.g., 90-minute meeting recording). Verify the service splits it into segments (e.g., 30-minute chunks), calls the real speech-to-text API for each segment, calls the real LLM to merge transcripts into a coherent summary, and outputs a single unified summary with coherent narrative, decisions, and action items. Failure: if segmentation doesn't occur, segments are processed out of order, coherence is lost, or the merging fails.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires real audio, real STT API, and real LLM to catch segmentation, ordering, API limit, and coherence failures.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording
behaviors.justification: This is the complete end-to-end pipeline with multiple real agentic steps (speech-to-text, summarization, and potential re-merging for long audio). Real failures include upload failures, API timeouts, malformed responses, missing error handling, orchestration gaps, and output format issues. Testing with mocks or single steps would not catch integration failures, timeout cascades, or orchestration breakdowns.
behaviors.test_description: Upload a real meeting recording through the service's intake endpoint. Verify audio is transcribed (real STT API), summarized (real LLM), structured JSON response with all required fields (attendees, decisions, action_items, narrative) is returned, and the process completes within a timeout threshold (e.g., 5 minutes for a 1-hour meeting). Failure: if any step fails silently, times out, returns malformed data, skips fields, or requires manual retry.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires the full pipeline with real APIs, proper orchestration, and timeout handling to catch end-to-end failures.
